title: Finding the Volume of a Rectangular Prism: 6 × 3 × 4

steps.instruction: Find the length, width, and height of the prism.
steps.work: Length = 6 cm, Width = 3 cm, Height = 4 cm.
steps.explanation: A rectangular prism has three dimensions. It does not matter which you call length, width, or height.
Volume measures how much space the shape takes up, in cubic units.

steps.instruction: Multiply two numbers first, then multiply by the third.
steps.explanation: You can multiply in any order. Grouping two easier numbers first can help.

steps.instruction: Write the volume with correct units.
steps.explanation: Volume is always measured in cubic units because it represents three dimensions.

title: Finding a Missing Dimension: V = 200 cubic in, l = 10, w = 5

steps.subgoalLabel: Write the formula with known values
steps.instruction: Substitute what you know into V = l × w × h.
steps.explanation: We know three of the four values, so we can solve for the missing one.
The base area (length times width) is 50 square inches.
The height is 4 inches. Check: 10 × 5 × 4 = 200 cubic inches.
